name: Sudarshana Chakra
description: The Sudarshana Chakra is a legendary spinning, disc-like weapon with 108 serrated edges, wielded by the Hindu god Vishnu. It is considered one of the most powerful weapons in Hindu mythology, embodying divine justice and cosmic order. Its origin is typically attributed to the celestial architect Vishwakarma, who crafted it from the sun's dust after the sun's heat became unbearable for the world. The Chakra possesses immense power, capable of instantly destroying enemies, protecting devotees, and upholding dharma (righteousness). It always returns to Vishnu after completing its task, symbolizing the cyclical nature of time and the inevitability of divine justice. The Sudarshana Chakra's spinning motion represents the continuous cycle of creation, preservation, and destruction. It is a symbol of Vishnu's supreme authority and his role as the preserver of the universe. The Chakra is often depicted with flames emanating from its edges, signifying its destructive power and divine energy. It is invoked in prayers and rituals for protection against evil forces and for the establishment of peace and harmony. The Sudarshana Chakra is not merely a weapon, but an embodiment of Vishnu's divine will and his unwavering commitment to righteousness.
subtitle: Vishnu's divine and powerful disc weapon
origin: Crafted by Vishwakarma from the sun's dust

keyMyths.title: Creation of the Chakra
keyMyths.description: Vishwakarma created the Sudarshana Chakra from the sun's dust to alleviate the world from its unbearable heat, gifting it to Vishnu. This act symbolizes sacrifice and the utilization of immense power for the greater good.

keyMyths.title: Krishna and the Chakra
keyMyths.description: During the Mahabharata war, Krishna wielded the Sudarshana Chakra to behead Shishupala, a king who had repeatedly insulted him. This exemplifies the Chakra's role in upholding righteousness and punishing evil.

keyMyths.title: Rama and the Chakra
keyMyths.description: In some accounts, Rama, an avatar of Vishnu, also wielded the Sudarshana Chakra, demonstrating its association with Vishnu's various incarnations and their divine duties. It reinforces the recurring theme of divine intervention against evil.
